name: Station 2: Daily Life and Music Station

artifacts.name: Gadur
artifacts.description: Gadur are ornate brass jars used by Maranao royalty for ceremonial gifts and storing precious items. They are recognized by their distinct pedestal bases and high-pointed lids that resemble mosque domes. These jars are masterfully crafted using the 'lost-wax' casting method in Tugaya.

artifacts.name: Kulintang
artifacts.description: The Kulintang is a musical instrument consisting of eight small, graduated brass gongs arranged horizontally on a wooden frame. It is the lead instrument in a Maranao ensemble and is played by striking the bosses with wooden sticks. It is essential for weddings, festivals, and royal welcomes.

artifacts.name: Baor
artifacts.description: A Baor is a hand-carved wooden chest, often made from Philippine mahogany and inlaid with mother-of-pearl (tipay). These chests were traditionally used to store the finest malongs and jewelry of a Maranao household. The carvings usually feature the pako rabong (growing fern) okir motif.
